figures.caption: Table 11: Sensitivity of DomLoRA to the LoRA rank on LLaMA-3.1-8B. α is set to 2r in all cases.
Table 10: Training efficiency on general instruction tuning. Shaded rows correspond to ✓ in Dom, where adapters are inserted only into the dominant adaptation module.
Figure 4: PAGE of all projection modules in LLaMA-3.1-8B-Instruct on Tulu.
Figure 5: PAGE of all projection modules in Qwen3-8B on MetaMathQA.

Figure 1: From broad to dominant placement. Vanilla LoRA places adapters across many layers and module types. (a) Layer-wise Reduction: fewer layers, all module types retained. (b) Module-type Reduction: fewer module types, all layers retained. (c) Reduction to a Single Dominant Module: PAGE is highly concentrated at one shallow FFN down-projection (magnified), suggesting that a single adapter placed there suffices.
Table 1: General instruction tuning against adapter-placement baselines. Shaded rows indicate DomLoRA, and bold marks the best result within each model group.
Figure 3: PAGE of all projection modules in Qwen3-8B on Tulu.
Table 3: LoRA variant results on general instruction tuning (LLaMA-3.1-8B-Instruct). Shaded rows correspond to ✓ in Dom, where adapters are inserted only into the dominant adaptation module.
Table 2: Reasoning, coding, and conversation results on Qwen3-8B and LLaMA-3.1-8B-Instruct.
Figure 9: PAGE of all projection modules in Qwen3-8B on WizardLM.
Figure 10: PAGE of all projection modules in LLaMA-3.1-8B-Instruct on WizardLM.

Table 8: Applying the dominant module placement method to LoRA variants on general instructiontuning benchmarks. Dom indicates whether adapters are inserted into the dominant adaptation module. bold marks the better result between the standard and Dom settings for each method and backbone.

Table 5: Ablation on dominant adaptation module selection (LLaMA-3.1-8B-Instruct). Wdown at Layer 1 is the dominant adaptation module used by DomLoRA. FFN-all adapts all three FFN projections (Wup,Wgate,Wdown), and Attn-all adapts all four attention projections (Wq,Wk,Wv,Wo).

Figure 2: PAGE across projection modules. (a)–(g) show all attention and FFN projections of Qwen3-8B, and (h) shows the FFN down-projection of LLaMA-3.1-8B-Instruct. Dashed vertical lines indicate the dominant adaptation module.
Figure 8: PAGE of all projection modules in LLaMA-3.1-8B-Instruct on Magicoder.
